label: CPL Linh Nguyen — Army, Vietnam, Agent Orange, General Discharge, Cannon Crewmember

dd214.name: Nguyen, Linh T.
dd214.decorations: Vietnam Service Medal, Vietnam Campaign Medal with 60 Device, National Defense Service Medal
dd214.dutyLocations: Fort Sill OK, Long Binh Vietnam, Camp Enari Vietnam, Phuoc Vinh Vietnam
dd214.remarks: Three years active duty with 24 months deployed to Vietnam 1969-1971. Artillery forward observer and cannon crewmember. Separated under General discharge status in 1971. Documented exposure to Agent Orange during defoliation operations in Phuoc Vinh and Long Binh areas.

vaRecords.provider: VA Los Angeles Health Care System
vaRecords.notes: Veteran age 62 with established Type 2 diabetes (diagnosed 2002) and hypertension. Reported Vietnam service 1969-1971 with potential Agent Orange exposure. VA intake includes screening for presumptive conditions.

EKG and stress test show evidence of CAD with anterior wall ischemia. Patient underwent cardiac catheterization showing 60% LAD stenosis. Stent placed. Attributed to diabetes and hypertension complications. Agent Orange presumptive condition evaluation ongoing.

Long-standing diabetes now with early kidney involvement (eGFR 55) and distal polyneuropathy. On insulin therapy plus metformin. Poor glycemic control despite medications. Related to presumptive Agent Orange exposure.

presumptiveExposures: Agent Orange (Vietnam 1969-1971, Phuoc Vinh and Long Binh areas)
summary.functionalImpact: Diabetes complications limit physical activity. Peripheral neuropathy affects gait and balance. Cardiac condition restricts exertion. Multiple daily medical appointments and medication management required.
